item.block1: Oil tankers transport some 2,000 million tonnes
item.block2: of crude oil around the world by sea.

item.block1: The IMO introduced measures to ensure that the majority
item.block2: of oil tankers are safely built and operated.
was built to cover the route from Europe to the Middle East round the Cape of Good Hope.

item.block1: The potential for oil to pollute the marine environment
item.block2: was officially recognised by the International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution of the Sea by Oil.
recognised that most oil pollution resulted from routine shipboard operations.

item.block1: At the time it was normal practice
item.block2: to wash the tanks out with water and then pump the resulting mixture of oil and water into the sea.
the dumping of oily wastes within a certain distance from land and in ‘special areas’.

item.block1: Although the OILPOL Convention had been ratified
item.block2: pollution control still remained a minor concern.
to wake up to the environmental consequences of an industrialised society.

item.block1: This resulted in the biggest oil pollution incident
item.block2: ever recorded up to that time.
